title: Uccāṭana-karma
century: Medieval (c. 7th–14th c.); commentarial tradition to 18th c.
description: The fifth of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the driving-away (*uccāṭana*) rite for forcing a hostile force to depart from a place, role, or relationship. High caution — the second of the "fierce four" *ugra*-ṣaṭ-karma (vidveṣaṇa, uccāṭana, māraṇa, plus the optional mohana). Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.
